channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: How Can Soccer Players Bend Their Shots in Midair?
item.description: As World Cup action kicks off, we look at the physics of the beautiful game.

item.title: The future of Hollywood isn’t feeding prompts into vanilla gen AI models
item.description: For all the noise that's been made about how generative AI is poised to revolutionize the filmmaking industry, there haven't really been any projects created with the technology that felt like the sort of entertainment people would pay to see. Most AI firms' video models are still only capable of churning out short bursts of […]

item.title: The FBI built its own replica small town to simulate real-world cyberattacks
item.description: Hidden inside a building in Alabama, the FBI has created its own small town as a dedicated cyber training ground for simulating cyberattacks.

item.title: Corsair Makr Pro 75 review: close to being damn near perfect
item.description: The Corsair Makr Pro 75 might just be the best gaming keyboard I've ever tested, if only the price were a touch lower and it had a numpad.

item.title: ‘Companies that can serve both human and agent audiences will be the ones that survive’: WordPress VIP CTO spells out the future of SEO, GEO and more
item.description: ‘For decades, we’ve all built websites for Google’: WordPress VIP’s CTO argues that publishers must optimize websites for both human readers and AI agents, without forgetting about trust.

item.title: NBA streetball, crafting with renewable energy and other new indie games worth checking out
item.description: Plus, the next game from the Mouthwashing devs and trying to survive as a sentient guitar.

item.title: The FCC Wants to Kill Burner Phones
item.description: Plus: AI bug hunting fuels Microsoft’s biggest-ever Patch Tuesday, ShinyHunters ransomware gang exploits an Oracle zero-day, and more.

item.title: EcoFlow PowerOcean Battery Review: Cutting My Bill in Half
item.description: Whether you want to buy cheaper electricity, store solar energy, or guard against outages, EcoFlow’s home battery might be just what you need.

item.title: Rivian’s CEO on Tesla’s Cybertruck, Ferrari’s Luce, and What Happens If the R2 Fails
item.description: RJ Scaringe, the CEO of Rivian Automotive, joined us for a wide-ranging interview about how his company’s new electric SUV fits into the current EV industry, and what comes next.

item.title: ICYMI: here's the week's 7 biggest tech news stories from WWDC 2026 to Trump's not-so-made-in-America phone
item.description: The week's biggest tech news from Nintendo, Apple, Trump Mobile, and more for June 13, 2026.

item.title: Test Dreo TurboCool 516S : le ventilateur connecté le plus petit, le plus rafraîchissant et le plus abordable de l’été
item.description: Encore peu connue en France, la marque Dreo compte bien se faire un nom cet été avec sa dernière gamme de ventilateurs et de climatiseurs connectés. Au printemps 2026, le constructeur chinois a dévoilé deux nouveaux modèles en France, dont le Dreo TurboCool 516S, aussi compact que rafraîchissant.

item.title: Google’s Pinpoint is the free research tool you should know about
item.description: Now available beyond journalists and academics, Pinpoint helps users make sense of giant piles of documents, emails, audio, video, and scans. This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps.

item.title: Insta360 Flow 2 : à moins de 63 €, ce gimbal capable de suivre un sujet tout seul est l’un des moins chers du moment
item.description: Taillé pour les créateurs de contenu qui n'ont pas un énorme budget, le stabilisateur pour smartphone Insta360 Flow 2 propose plusieurs fonctionnalités intelligentes bien pratiques. Actuellement, Joybuy le propose à 62,99 euros au lieu de 109,99 euros.
